summary: I was born in Burma, but life there was very difficult because of the ongoing civil war between ethnic rebel groups and the Burmese military regime. Burmese soldiers often came to our village, burning houses and harming people, which made my family live in constant fear. In 2003, due to this violence and instability, my family decided to leave Burma to find peace and safety.

After leaving Burma in 2003, my family and I moved to the Mae La Oon refugee camp in Thailand. Most of the refugees there were Karen people like us, and the camp was safer than where we had lived before. In Mae La Oon, I attended school alongside my siblings, and we had access to hospitals supported by NGOs and the United Nations, which helped improve our lives during that time.

Thailand became our home when we fled Burma in 2003 and stayed at the Mae La Oon refugee camp. Living there was better than in Burma because we had access to education and healthcare provided by international organizations. Although it was still a challenging life, Thailand gave us a chance to recover and prepare for a better future.

The United Nations played an important role in supporting us while we were in the Mae La Oon refugee camp in Thailand. They helped establish schools and hospitals, which allowed me and my siblings to get an education and receive medical care. Their support made a big difference in improving our quality of life during those difficult years.

In 2013, my family moved from the refugee camp in Thailand to the United States, which marked a major change in our lives. Living here has been both challenging and hopeful, especially as we adjust to a new language and culture. Despite difficulties like learning English, I am grateful for the opportunities to study and build a better future without the fear we had in Burma.

Now, I live in Saint Paul, Minnesota with my family after moving here from the refugee camp. Life here is different and sometimes hard because of language barriers, but I am happy to attend school and meet new friends. Saint Paul has given us safety and hope, and I feel grateful that we can live peacefully without worrying about the dangers we faced before.
